proof_trace: parse_msg() can return NULL in multiple conditions (descriptor NULL, allocation failure, reflection NULL) but pb2json() dereferences the result without NULL checks at lines 524-525. json2string() also dereferences the NULL pointer at line 516.
attack_scenario: Attacker sends malformed protobuf message causing GetDescriptor() to return NULL, leading to parse_msg() returning NULL, which pb2json() passes to json2string() causing segmentation fault.

proof_trace: ResolveAddress functions accept RpcEndpoint* parameter but unconditionally dereference it at line 42 (*endpoint = it->endpoint()) without NULL validation. All ResolveAddress variants eventually reach this vulnerable dereference.
attack_scenario: Attacker calls RpcClientImpl::ResolveAddress with nullptr as second parameter, causing segmentation fault when code dereferences the NULL pointer at rpc_endpoint.cc:42.
reason: NPD — unconditional dereference of RpcEndpoint* without NULL check。PR #251提出済み(OPEN)。メンテナ反応なし。待ち。

proof_trace: field2json() can return NULL when repeated=false and field type is MESSAGE (line 214 calls parse_msg which returns NULL for invalid descriptors). parse_msg() dereferences the result immediately at line 270 (*field_json) without any NULL check.
attack_scenario: Attacker provides protobuf message with nested MESSAGE field having invalid/corrupted descriptor, causing parse_msg() to return NULL which gets dereferenced in the calling parse_msg(), resulting in segmentation fault.

proof_trace: Function can return NULL if invalid CompressType hits default case and SCHECK(false) returns instead of terminating (e.g. with custom log handler). Callers directly use return value in scoped_ptr without NULL checking before calling os->Flush().
attack_scenario: Attacker sends network message with invalid compression type value → SCHECK(false) returns → NULL returned → NULL pointer dereference crash in os->Flush().
